Ms. Daugherty stated that as to the Nixon Trust her clients are the beneficiaries, that the Cottage Trust was established for three Brothers that have issues, that the Cottage Trust is to go to a Niece (Justine Tico), and that it is unclear as to what funds were diverted to the Cottage Trust. In addition, Ms. Daugherty stated that they want an accounting, that Ms. Tico has Counsel, that she doesn't know if she'll join, and that they don't know if the assets were co-mingled or diverted to the trust. Mr. Gearinger stated that there is no basis for doing an accounting of the Cottage Grove Trust. Ms. Daugherty stated that they want an accounting for both Trusts, that $2,000,000.00 disappeared, that they are in the beginning of discovery, that Patrick Dana loaned his wife $400,000.00, and that there are concerns. Mr. Gearinger stated that his clients agreed to do an accounting of the Al Nixon Trust, that they want the account period from when he was named in 2005 through the death of decedent. Arguments regarding the account period were held. Mr. Grabstein stated that his response was filed this morning. Mr. Gearinger stated that his client was the only Successor Trustee upon the death of decedent and that his client did have a power of attorney. The Court stated that Mr. Gearinger wished to file some authorities regarding an earlier account period and that it sounds as if there is a stipulation as to the accounting from the date of death forward which will be on file by 12/13/10. Ms. Daugherty's brief is due 11-9-10. Mr. Gearingers brief is due 11/16/10. Matter continued to 11/30/10 at 10:30 a.m. for the Court to determine whether there will be an earlier account.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

Petition For Financial Elder Abuse
